pattern: Total travel time is monotone decreasing in speed, so binary search the smallest integer speed whose ceil-rounded ride times fit within the hour budget.
hints: If a given speed is fast enough, any faster speed also works, which means the feasible speeds form a suffix you can binary search.
All rides except the last must finish at an integer hour, so each of those takes ceil(dist[i]/speed) hours; the last takes the exact fraction dist[-1]/speed.

Rule out impossibility first: if the number of rides exceeds the floor of hour you can never finish; otherwise binary search speed in a bounded range using exact rational comparison to dodge floating error.

edge_inputs.description: impossible because not enough whole hours

stress.description: random distances with a generous integer-plus-fraction hour budget that keeps it feasible
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    dist = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    # generous budget: each ride at most a few hours plus slack, two decimals
    hour = n + 5 + rng.randint(0, 99) / 100.0
    return [dist, hour]
